_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to use comprehension strategies to compare and analyse ideas across texts, and to deepen our understanding through critical thinking.

_source.w: We are learning to use comprehension strategies to compare and analyse ideas across texts, and to deepen our understanding through critical thinking.
y: Comparing ideas across texts develops our ability to think critically and synthesise information.
These strategies help us build deep understanding of complex topics.
Analytical comprehension is the reading skill that drives all advanced learning.
eg: We compare how different texts present the same topic and analyse the differences in perspective.
We use questioning strategies to interrogate a text — 'Why did the author choose to include this? What is missing?'.
We synthesise information from multiple sources to build a comprehensive understanding of a topic.
We connect ideas across texts, identifying patterns, contradictions and complementary information.
We evaluate the strength of arguments and evidence presented in different texts.
We discuss our analytical findings with others, refining our thinking through dialogue.
